feed.title: SAP Community - SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
feed.subtitle: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Error related to period determination occurred while creating an asset card for a US company
entry.content:
US company code: 3010
Custom asset class: Z001
Depreciation key used under US GAAP: SUL3

The system has enabled the configurable fiscal year variant SZ and assigned it to company code 3010. However, when creating an asset card for company code 3010 with custom asset class Z001, an error occurs. What could be the cause?

For detailed configuration information, please refer to the screenshots in the attachment.

The error message is as follows:

“Period determination is not possible on date 20260708 for period control SA
Diagnosis
For depreciation key (with period control SA and period control method of phase), no depreciation period could be determined for date 20260708.”

entry.title: **Title:** [S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition] Is there an API to post an intracompany asset transfer (As
entry.content:
**Title:** [S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition] Is there an API to post an intracompany asset transfer (Asset-to-Asset / ABUMN equivalent)?

**Body:**

I'm working on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (2602) and want to perform an intracompany asset transfer (asset A → asset B within the same company code, i.e. the equivalent of transaction ABUMN) from an external system via API.

Here's what I've found so far:

- **Fixed Asset - Master Data** (OData V4, `api_fixedasset`) — master data CRUD only, no transfer transaction.
- **Journal Entry - Post** (SOAP, communication scenario SAP_COM_0002 / `JournalEntryBulkCreateRequest`) — can post journal entries, but it's unclear how to reproduce the transfer-specific logic (proportional net book value transfer across all depreciation areas).

My questions:

1. Is there a **standard released API** (OData or SOAP) to post an intracompany asset transfer (ABUMN equivalent)?
2. If so, **which API / communication scenario** should I use?
3. If possible, could you share a **sample payload** for a transfer such as:
- Transfer within the same company code
- Sending asset: `6100000-1`
- Receiving asset: `83000000-1`

If no dedicated API exists, I'd also appreciate guidance on the recommended approach for replicating this via the Journal Entry API (e.g. which asset transaction type to use).

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Unable to auto create POs from source assigned PRs in case of stock transport order bet two Sloc
entry.content:
Hi Team,
We are having a scenario where we carryout stock transport order between two storage locations using the document type UD. The process begins with running a MRP and generating STO PR's on the supplying storage location. Now, these PR's are to be converted to PO using "schedule purchasing job" (automatic conversion of PR to PO). Since the PR's are assigned with both supplying storage location and plant, we expect this to be converted to PO automatically. However, this is not happening. When we maintain source list with same plant as supplying plant, system does create auto PO. However, for some of the products when we are trying to maintain the source list, it gives an error "Active Source List exists for Material: NAU98113G Plant: GP01 Combination.". This is because we already have maintained the sourcelist for external suppliers for our regular purchases (Please note, this is NOT a fixed source of supply). 
So, can you kindly let us know how can we maintain the sourcelist for both external suppliers and supplying plant (for STO's). If this is not possible, then why would system not convert the PR to PO automatically even when required source exists on the PR.

Thanks.

entry.title: Direct Email out of Customer Invoices in Financial Accounting (FI)
entry.content:
Dear Community , 
as soon as we create Customer Invoices in Financial Accounting (FI) we want to send the PDF output directly to a dedicated email-adresss. These customer invoices are created via API interface. 
We do not want to create manually a correspondence of type SAP21 (FI Customer Invoice ) as described Printing of Customer Invoices in Financial Accounting (FI) | SAP Help Portal 

The procedure is described in help topic: Configuring the Output of FI Customer Invoices | SAP Help Portal 

Please advice how to setup the automatically email output of correspondence type SAP21

Propably  we have to implement https://me.sap.com/notes/3757894  - nevertheless our understaing is, that the email output should be generated automatically for manually created documents

entry.title: App Display MRP Master Data Issues - UOM conversion message
entry.content:
In Plant FR30, currently there are two components which have the Base unit of measure = KM and are managed in MM in the Bill of materials component.

While checking the consistency of the data in the app “Display MRP Master Data Issues” the message “Unit of measure MM in KM cannot be converted” appears.

All the Standard configurations exists for the UOMs (KM , M and MM) and the conversion are fine as well.

Please let us know how to resolve this issue and let us know the impact of this message if not resolved.

entry.title: Multiple reservation items for a service order
entry.content:
Hello experts,

We are implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Service Line of Business, and we have a question regarding the creation of reservations for stock service parts.

During the execution of the service process, when we release a service order with multiple stock service part items, the system creates an individual reservation for each service part item, as shown below:

 

In this example, we created a service order with two stock service parts, and the system generated two separate reservations instead of one reservation with two items.

I have read that this is the standard behavior in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, but I would like to confirm if this is the expected design.

Our concern is that we will have service orders containing several service parts, and if the system creates one reservation per service part item, we may end up managing a very high number of reservation documents.

I have checked the CBC configuration but could not find any option to group reservations by service order. Is there any configuration, best practice, or workaround to reduce the number of reservations created for service orders?

Thank you in advanced.

Best regards,

David Avila.

entry.title: Tengo 3 consultas que realizar sobre modulo de Manufactura de SAP H4HANA CLOUD
entry.content:
Buenas tardes Equipo,

Gusto en saludarles.

Les escribo por las siguientes consultas.

Estoy por iniciar un proceso de implementacion y me embargan 3 consultas relevantes para continuar. 

Se pueden realizar las siguientes funciones en SAP S4HANA Cloud public:

  1. Definir clases de orden de fabricación. Se pueden crear nuevas y configurar segun la necesidad?
  2. Definir clave para el valor prefijado. se pueden crear nuevas y configurar segun la necesidad?
  3. Crear ordenes de fabricación sin material que se asocien a un elemento PEP?

Quedo atento a sus respuestas.

Saludos,

NR

entry.title: Putaway WT Creation to Consider Storage Bin capacity Including Existing Stock and Open WTs
entry.content:
Hi,

We would like to check whether the below putaway requirement can be achieved using standard SAP EWM functionality.

During automatic putaway Warehouse Task creation, the system should consider the maximum allowed weight/load capacity of a storage bin group or bay. The maximum weight of the storage group/bay load should be treated as the cumulative weight capacity of all bins assigned to that storage group/bay.

Business Requirement

During automatic WT creation for putaway, the system should evaluate each destination bin proposed during the storage bin search sequence and perform the following checks:

  1. Identify the destination storage bin proposed by the system during putaway search.
  2. Determine the storage group/bay associated with that bin.
  3. Identify all other storage bins belonging to the same storage group/bay.
  4. Check whether there are already pallets/HUs physically present in those bins.
  5. Check whether there are any open Warehouse Tasks directed to any of the bins within the same storage group/bay.
  6. Calculate the total expected load of the storage group/bay as follows:

Total Load =Current stock/HU weight already present in all bins of the storage group+ Weight of open Warehouse Tasks already directed to bins in the same storage group+ Weight of the current pallet/HU being put away

  1. Compare this calculated total load against the maintained maximum allowed weight/load capacity of the storage group/bay.

Expected System Behavior:

If the calculated total load is greater than the maximum allowed weight of the storage group/bay:

System should not create the automatic putaway Warehouse Task.An error/exception should be raised.

 If the calculated total load is less than or equal to the maximum allowed weight of the storage group/bay: 

System should allow creation of the putaway Warehouse Task. 

 This logic should be applied to each and every bin considered by the system during the putaway search sequence execution, before the system finalizes the destination bin and creates the Warehouse Task. 

 

Example Logic

If a storage group/bay has multiple bins assigned to it, the system should not only check the individual bin capacity, but should also check the overall load capacity of the complete storage group/bay.

Additionally, the system should also consider open Warehouse Tasks already created toward the bins in that same storage group/bay, because these tasks represent future expected stock/load.

Clarification Required from SAP

Could you please confirm whether this requirement can be achieved using standard SAP EWM Public cloud functionality?

Specifically, we would like to know:

  1. Is there any standard configuration available in SAP EWM to check the maximum cumulative weight of a storage bin group/bay during automatic putaway WT creation?
  2. Can standard putaway strategy consider not only the current stock in the bins, but also the open Warehouse Tasks directed to those bins while calculating available load capacity?
  3. Is there any standard enhancement/BAdI recommended by SAP to implement this logic during destination bin determination or putaway WT creation?
  4. If this is not supported in standard, could SAP please confirm whether this requirement must be handled through a custom enhancement?

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Where is the customer group maintained in management allocation?
entry.content:
When performing allocation, I want to use customer and revenue account as the allocation basis. However, customer numbers are not sequential, so I need to group/bundle customers. However, I am not sure how to create a customer group that can be used in management allocation. Could you please advise on how to create it?

The usage scenario for management allocation is:

  • Allocation Context: Margin Analysis

  • Allocation Type: Top‑Down Distribution

I have tried creating a group in 'Manage Global Hierarchies' with Type 'Customer Hierarchy – Analytics', but it still does not appear in management allocation.

entry.title: Request for details on SAP Upgrade 2608 changes.
entry.content: The user is requesting detailed information about SAP Upgrade 2608. Additional context shows interest in changes to Asset accounting, WBS Elements, and other upgrade features.

entry.title: Validate G/L account in Account Assignment in Goods Issue with Movement Type 201
entry.content:
In the current process, users post Goods Issue using Movement Type 201, where a single cost center is used with multiple G/L accounts. Since the standard SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud system does not support configuring multiple G/L accounts for a single cost center, users are manually selecting the required G/L account, which increases the risk of incorrect postings. To avoid manual errors and improve process efficiency, an automation or validation is required to determine or validate the appropriate G/L account based on predefined business rules, ensuring that only the correct G/L account is posted against the respective cost center.

How can we achieve this ?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Confirmation Required: Released API/CDS View for Customer Hierarchy Change Documents
entry.content:
Hello SAP Support Team,We are currently designing an outbound integration from SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud to an external system to send Customer Hierarchy data.As there is no standard API available for Customer Hierarchies, our current approach is to create a custom CDS view based on the standard CDS views:

  • I_CustUnivHierarchy
  • I_CustUnivHierarchyNode


This custom CDS view would then be exposed as a custom API to the external system.However, we have a delta requirement for this interface. The external system should receive only:

  • Newly created hierarchy records
  • Updated hierarchy records
  • Deleted hierarchy records


The challenge is that the CDS views I_CustUnivHierarchy and I_CustUnivHierarchyNode do not appear to provide change tracking information, such as creation date, last changed date, deletion indicator, or change type.In the Manage Customer Hierarchies Fiori app, we can see change history information

entry.title: Can't access Hands-on Practice for Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Hi Team,

until last week I was able to access the "Hands-on Practice for Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition" practice system. However, since Monday I re-enrolled it no longer works. I also tried to access the other practice systems without success.

When I click on "Access", I am redirected to another link and the following error message appears: Selected user account does not exist in tenant 'SAP SE' and cannot access the application 'https://accounts.sap.com/msec' in that tenant. The account needs to be added as an external user in the tenant first. Please use a different account.

 

I have taken the following actions:

  • Cleared the browser’s cache, history, and cookies (All time) and then, restarted the browser.

  • Used an Incognito/Private browsing window and Tried a different browser I haven't used before.
  • Disabled any active (corporate/personal) VPN, proxy, or network filtering software.
  • Tried accessing the practice system from a different network (home network and mobile hotspot).

But it still doesn't work.

The same error message is also displayed for other colleagues.
 
Would anyone be able to help regarding this issue, please?

entry.title: How to migrate Revision Level using the Inspection Plan migration object in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public
entry.content:
Question

Hello SAP Community,

We are currently working on a data migration project in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and need to migrate a large number of Inspection Plans.

During our analysis of the standard migration object "Inspection Plan", we noticed that the migration template does not seem to contain any field for the Revision Level.

However, when an Inspection Plan is created manually, the Revision Level can be maintained in the header validity section.

Business Requirement

Our customer manages Inspection Plans based on engineering revisions. Each Inspection Plan is linked to a specific Revision Level, and this information is required to ensure proper version control and selection of the correct inspection plan during business processes.

As part of the data migration, we need to migrate:

  • Inspection Plan header data
  • Operations and characteristics
  • Associated Revision Level

Questions

  1. Is the Revision Level supported by the standard Inspection Plan migration object in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?
  2. If yes, where should it be maintained in the migration template?
  3. If not, what is the recommended approach to migrate Inspection Plans with Revision Levels?
  4. Has anyone successfully implemented a workaround or alternative process for this requirement?

I have attached a screenshot showing the Revision Level field available during manual Inspection Plan creation.

Any feedback or experience would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance.

Best regards,

entry.title: Calculation behind the Days Sales Outstanding and the Days Payable Outstanding apps
entry.content:
I am looking for a detailed documentaion on the how the figures are calculated for the 2 below apps, for them the outcome is not satisfying and they want more information on the calculation behind 

App - Days Sales Outstanding

 App - Days Payable Outstanding

entry.title: Profit Center Derivation for System-Generated Withholding Tax Line Items in Supplier Invoice Posting
entry.content:
We would like to request confirmation regarding the standard behavior of Profit Center derivation for system-generated withholding tax (WHT) line items during Supplier Invoice posting in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

Business Requirement

Our client would like all Withholding Tax Payable postings generated during Supplier Invoice posting to be assigned to a single Head Office (HO) Profit Center, regardless of the Profit Centers assigned to the expense line items.

The objective is to centralize all withholding tax liabilities under the Head Office Profit Center to facilitate tax monitoring, reconciliation, and remittance.

Current System Behavior

During Supplier Invoice posting, the withholding tax payable line item inherits the Profit Center from the originating expense line(s). Consequently, if the invoice contains expenses assigned to multiple Profit Centers, the generated withholding tax liability is also split across those Profit Centers.

This appears to be standard system behavior due to document splitting and standard Profit Center derivation.

Investigation Performed

We have reviewed the standard Manage Substitution and Validation Rules application and noted that SAP provides the standard substitution rule /0SAP/SetProfitCenter for Profit Center derivation.

Based on our understanding and our review of SAP Knowledge Base Articles, it appears that:

  • Profit Center is derived during the standard posting process.
  • Once the Profit Center has been determined, it cannot be overwritten for system-generated journal entry line items.
  • The withholding tax payable line participates in document splitting and therefore inherits the originating account assignments.

Request for Confirmation

Could you please confirm the following:

  1. Is it standard SAP behavior that system-generated withholding tax line items inherit the Profit Center from the originating expense line items?
  2. Is there any standard configuration, SSCUI, Business Configuration, BAdI, or supported extensibility option in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud that allows assigning the Profit Center of the withholding tax payable line item to a fixed Profit Center (e.g., Head Office) during Supplier Invoice posting?
  3. If this requirement is not supported, could you please confirm that this is a product limitation by design so that we can communicate the standard behavior to our client?

Any reference to relevant SAP documentation or Knowledge Base Articles would also be appreciated.

Thank you for your assistance.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: EBRR S/4HANA Public Cloud — WIP Clearing Zero Invoice Posts Without EBRR Completing in Same Period
entry.content:
We are running SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud and have identified an
issue with Event-Based Revenue Recognition (EBRR) related to
zero invoices used for WIP clearing purposes.

SCENARIO:
- Zero invoice created on 2 July with backdated posting date of 30 June
- Final EBRR batch for June had already completed before invoice was created
- System posted invoice entries in June with no warning or block
- EBRR recognition entry posted in July instead of June
- Result: WIP and revenue misstated across two periods requiring
manual journal entry correction

INCORRECT JOURNAL ENTRIES POSTED:

At invoice creation (posting date 30 June):
DR Revenue / CR WIP — posted in June

When EBRR batch ran in July:
CR Revenue / DR WIP — posted in July

EXPECTED BEHAVIOR:
The system should either block the invoice from posting if EBRR
cannot complete in the same period, OR trigger an immediate
document-level EBRR run upon invoice creation.

QUESTIONS:
1. Should the system block a zero WIP clearing invoice from posting
if EBRR cannot complete in the same period?
2. Can EBRR be configured to trigger at document level upon invoice
creation rather than batch only?
3. Is there a configuration to lock the billing period immediately
after the final EBRR batch run?
4. Is this a known gap in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud EBRR? Are there
any SAP Notes addressing this?

Any guidance from the community or SAP product team would be
greatly appreciated.

entry.title: customer invoices are not visible in E-document cockpit for e-invoicing germany
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

we are implementing e-invoicing germany for customer :

after creating invoices we are not able to see any documents flow in e-document cockpit app.

all connections are setup already we can only see poland country invoices nothing related to german invoices

entry.title: Change of Customer number in Master Data
entry.content:
We have a customer with the number and the customer name is correct, But we want the customer number to be changed. We have a sales order for that customer now but can we change the customer number in backend code from SAP side because our client wants to change the number from the next transaction onwards. Is it possible to do the same.?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Approval process completed without Completion of all approvals
entry.content:
In the non-sequential approval when the approval group has more than 1 approver. The approval must be provided by every member of the approval group.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.
